title: Arlong Puts a Price on the Village
scene: On pages 13–16, Nojiko’s account of the past reveals how Nami, Bellemere, and Cocoyasi Village were damaged by Arlong’s arrival. The flashback reaches Arlong’s arrival, his money demands, and Bellemere’s impossible choice.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Let's go, Nami! It's fine as long as it means Bellie- mere will be saved!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'll be able to save my two children...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: That money is my children's share.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: I don't have enough to pay for myself.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: even if it means I'll lose my life...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I... I just can't bring myself to say that I don't have a family.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: How could you do such a stupid thing!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Even if it's just mere words, I still want to be called as their mother.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: those girls... are my children, aren't they?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I... I wish I were adopted by a rich family!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I really wanted to buy you girls a lot of things...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Whether it be new books or new clothes!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: for not ever being able to do anything motherly for you girls...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: That's not true! We don't need anything so please don't die!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: So please stay with us forever!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Aren't you a former marine, belliemere? You're strong, right? You should be able to beat these guys up no problem!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I still haven't shown you the map of the world I'll draw!

summary: This part deepens Nami’s backstory, showing how family, money, and survival became tied to Arlong’s control over the village.
